user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Iron Creek Mining.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 12:28 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 15:59 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 11:15 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:08:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
